---
title: "Circle Flow"
---

The `.circle-flow` style arranges items as nodes on a ring, connected by arrows. It is the original style and the namesake of the extension.

## Basic usage

````markdown
::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::
````

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

## Item count

The layout automatically adjusts to any number of items. With a single item there is no arrow.

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
:::

With two items, arrows are offset so they don't overlap.

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
:::

Three or more items are arranged evenly around the circle.

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
Dave
:::
:::

Node size scales down automatically as more items are added.

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
Dave
:::
::: item
Ethan
:::
:::

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
Dave
:::
::: item
Ethan
:::
::: item
Fiona
:::
:::

## Annotations

Give any item a nested `.annotation` div to attach an external callout that
points at its node with a leader line. The annotation content is free-form
Markdown; any bold text or heading is tinted to match the node color, which is
handy for a title (but optional). Annotate all, some, or none of the nodes.

Nest the fenced divs with **more colons on the outer fences** (`:::::` for the
chart, `::::` for each item, `:::` for the annotation) and leave a blank line
around the inner fences.

````markdown
::::: circle-flow

:::: item
Plan

::: annotation
**Plan** define scope, goals, and success metrics up front
:::

::::

:::: item
Build

::: annotation
**Build** implement the work in small, reviewable increments
:::

::::

:::: item
Ship

::: annotation
**Ship** release to users and monitor for issues
:::

::::

:::::
````

::::: circle-flow

:::: item
Plan

::: annotation
**Plan** define scope, goals, and success metrics up front
:::

::::

:::: item
Build

::: annotation
**Build** implement the work in small, reviewable increments
:::

::::

:::: item
Ship

::: annotation
**Ship** release to users and monitor for issues
:::

::::

:::::

The leader lines can be restyled (dashed, dotted, removed) and the colors
adjusted — see the [Annotations](annotations.qmd) concept page for the full set
of `ann-line`, `ann-line-color`, and `ann-title-color` options, which work on
every annotated layout.

## What's next

- [Node Shapes](node-shapes.qmd) — change how individual nodes look
- [Arrow Types](arrow-types.qmd) — change how items are connected
- [Colors](colors.qmd) — control node and arrow colors
